title: Arch of Constantine
description: This quiz is for the video Arch of Constantine.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Arch of Constantine is [[☃ dropdown 1]] in that it celebrates a victory of a Roman, Constantine, over another Roman, Maxentius, at the Battle of the Milvian Bridge.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7551104f9b41acaee806efe02b6507a2.png)
hints:
**Arches typically celebrated a ruler's military victory over another country and the Arch of Constantine is unusual because it celebrates the victory of one Roman over another.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-rome/v/arch-of-constantine--315-c-e)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Artists borrowed stone from monuments to previous emperors and re-carved the faces of those emperors with the face of Constantine.**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7551104f9b41acaee806efe02b6507a2.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**This is correct. The Arch of Constantine does borrow stone from the monuments to previous emperors with the faces re-carved to look like Constantine.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-rome/v/arch-of-constantine--315-c-e)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Arch of Constantine borrowed elements from other monuments to show that Constantine was a good emperor just like  [[☃ dropdown 1]] who represented a golden age.**


![Picture of the Arch of Constantine](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7551104f9b41acaee806efe02b6507a2.png)
hints:
**Trajan, Hadrian, and Marcus Aurelius were considered good and successful emperors of Rome. The others in the list were not.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-rome/v/arch-of-constantine--315-c-e)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The style of the frieze sculptures moves away from [[☃ dropdown 1]] of earlier Roman art and moves toward [[☃ dropdown 2]].**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2229ed414b49f77f84dbcd34a0145dc9.png)
hints:
**The style of the frieze sculpture moves away from the naturalism and classicism of earlier Roman art and moving toward a more medieval abstraction.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-400-c-e--ancient-cultures-1/ancient-rome/v/arch-of-constantine--315-c-e)**
